reference.paper: W.-S. Ko, D.-H. Kim, Y.-J. Kwon, M. Lee (2018), Metals 8(11), 900

bulk.max_diff: <1e-4 Ang for a/c, <1e-4 eV/atom for E

elastic_constants_GPa.verdict: all 6 independent Cij match NIST IPR within numerical precision

surface_energies_mJ_m2.note: likely different termination from NIST IPR; will revisit

summary.interpretation: 5/7 faces reproduce NIST IPR predicted values within 0.1 mJ/m^2. Bulk lattice/elastic constants match within numerical precision. This confirms correct LAMMPS-Ko2018 MEAM implementation. Surface gamma differences in (001) and (111) persist after testing: (a) multiple z-shifts of the bulk before slab cleavage, (b) ASE surface() with shifts and parity changes (L=11/12/13), (c) pymatgen SlabGenerator with varied min_slab_size (12/18/24 Ang), min_vacuum_size (18/25 Ang), and symmetrize options. In all cases pymatgen finds only ONE unique termination per face, which is consistent with the I4_1/amd symmetry that makes all four atoms in the conventional cell equivalent. The (001) gamma converges to ~485 mJ/m^2 and the (111) to ~472 mJ/m^2 across thickness/vacuum variations. The NIST IPR (001)=395.47 mJ/m^2 likely reflects a different slab construction or relaxation in atomman (NIST's internal property pipeline) that we have not been able to reproduce. Validation of the potential implementation is considered complete.
next_steps: Optional: install atomman (NIST's calculator) to attempt to reproduce the (001) value exactly
Integrate these MEAM values into existing comparison tables (figures/fig3_cij_comparison.tif and surface energy comparison) with the (001) discrepancy footnoted
